sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Let me check what happened after moving RIGHT twice. The player should now be at cols 20-39, overlapping with the blocks at cols 36-39. Let me see if anything special happened.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check player position and if overlapping blocks caused any change
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('b', 'c', 'M', 'N'):
        print(f"{n['color']}: pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Check if blocks changed or disappeared
print(f"\nTimer N pixels: {[n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color']=='N'][0]['pixels']}")
</parameter>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The player is now at cols 20-39, overlapping with the blocks at cols 36-39. But nothing special happened - blocks are still there, player still there. Just timer decreased from 62 to 58.
So overlapping blocks doesn't trigger anything. Let me think about other possibilities:
1. Maybe I need to move UP to reach the blocks (they're at rows 1-7, player is at rows 16-19)
2. Maybe I need to use MOUSE on the yellow containers
3. Maybe I need to move the player into the yellow containers
The yellow containers are at rows 52-59, but the player can only go DOWN to row 44. So the player can't reach them.
Let me try moving UP to get closer to the blocks vertically. The blocks are at rows 1-7, and the player can go UP to row 12. So the player can get close but not overlap vertically.
Wait - maybe I should try MOUSE on the yellow containers instead. Let me try clicking on one of them.
